mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate inversely impacts equity valuations through the discount rate channel. A lower FEDFUNDS rate reduces the cost of capital and increases the present value of future cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns. This mechanism operates continuously across all market conditions, with the strength of the signal proportional to the rate level.
evidence_refs: fact:Monetary policy transmission to equity prices is well-documented
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC is negative or below 0.03 over any 3-year window; or the Sharpe ratio of long-short portfolios based on FEDFUNDS deciles is not significantly different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds a critical threshold of 5.5%, it signals significant labor market slack that precedes a downturn in corporate earnings and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The threshold is based on historical NBER recession indicators.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 5.5% and rising over two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Historical recessions are often preceded by UNRATE rising above 5.5%
failure_condition: If SPX forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE > 5.5%, or if the average return in triggered periods is not statistically negative (one-sided t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the federal funds rate of more than 25 basis points in a single month indicates a hawkish Fed surprise that tightens financial conditions and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:Surprise rate hikes historically cause short-term equity selloffs
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return following a 25bp+ hike is not negative (p>0.05) or if more than 40% of such events yield positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: When both inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) are elevated above critical levels (inflation > 200, rates > 4.0), the economy faces stagflationary pressures that severely compress valuations. The joint condition is more predictive than either series alone, as it captures the central bank's forced tightening in an overheated environment.
evidence_refs: fact:1970s stagflation episodes showed severe equity declines
failure_condition: If the average forward 20-day return when both conditions are met is not significantly more negative than when only one condition is met (two-sample t-test p>0.05), or if the interaction term in a linear regression is not negative and significant.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising unemployment is interpreted positively by markets because it signals cooling labor demand, reducing the need for further rate hikes and thus alleviating valuation pressure. Thus, increases in UNRATE during tightening are associated with positive forward returns, opposite to the normal negative relationship.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has increased over the past 6 months (tightening regime) and UNRATE rises by more than 0.1pp in the month
evidence_refs: fact:In 2018 tightening cycle, rising unemployment was initially welcomed by markets
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return during tightening-regime UNRATE increases is not positive (p>0.05), or if the regime interaction coefficient is not significant in a regression with 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: The level of industrial production (INDPRO) is a leading indicator of economic output. Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing and corporate profits, which supports higher equity prices. The effect is continuous over monthly horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a component of the Conference Board Leading Economic Index
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of INDPRO level on 5-day forward returns is negative or below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 2,000,000 units (annualized), it indicates a significant contraction in the housing sector, which historically precedes broader economic recessions and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing leads economic downturns by 4-6 quarters (Leamer, 2007)
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return when HOUST < 2000 is not negative (p>0.05) or if the signal has less than 60% directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales (RSAFS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates robust consumer demand and household confidence, driving corporate earnings and leading to positive S&P 500 returns. The combination is more powerful than either signal alone because it confirms genuine economic momentum rather than temporary shocks.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% and UNRATE month-over-month change < -0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes ~70% of US GDP
failure_condition: If the interaction term in a regression of 20-day returns on RSAFS growth and UNRATE change is not significantly positive, or if the average return when both conditions are met is not higher than when only one condition is met (t-test p>0.05).
